```{r data_filter_ccp_mod}
   dplyr::select(protein_id, peptide_id, filename, intensity)%>%
as.data.table()

```

## 4c. create TRACE ANNOTATION table

```{r Fetch_uniprot for traceAnnotation df}
uniprot <- fetch_uniprot(unique(data_filter_ccp_mod$protein_id),
                         columns = c("protein_name", "length", "sequence", "mass", "gene_names","organism_name","xref_geneid",
    "xref_string", "go_f", "go_p", "go_c", "cc_interaction", "ft_act_site", "ft_binding",
    "cc_cofactor", "cc_catalytic_activity", "xref_pdb"),
    batchsize = 50) 
```

```{r traceAnnotation_table}
## Trace annotation table

## information from uniprot
traceAnnotation <- as.data.table(uniprot) %>%
  dplyr::rename("Mass" ="mass") # there is a bug in the annotateTraces function, it doesn't add the protein_mw column if the mass column is "mass" and not "Mass". Tried to fix the function but not fully managed: it does add the column, but put NAs as values....

head(traceAnnotation, n = 2)
```

## 4d. Get traces list

```{r pepTraces_raw}

samples <- unique(fraction_annotation_table$sample)


# to order by number in the string
if(sample_sort_by_number == T) {
samples <- str_sort(samples, numeric = TRUE)}


traces_list <- lapply(samples,function(x){
  message(x)
  ann <- subset(fraction_annotation_sub, sample==x) 
  ann <- subset(ann, filename %in% data_filter_ccp_mod$filename) 
  ann <- subset(ann, select = c("filename","fraction_number")) 
  setkey(ann,fraction_number) 
  data_sub_select <- subset(data_filter_ccp_mod,filename %in% ann$filename) 
  data_sub_select <- merge(data_sub_select,ann, by=c("filename")) 
  setkey(data_sub_select,fraction_number) 
  data_sub_select[,fraction_number:=NULL] 
  traces <- importPCPdata(input_data=data_sub_select,fraction_annotation=ann)
  return(traces)
})


names(traces_list) = samples
class(traces_list) <- "tracesList"


#' # Annotate traces with information from uniprot
 
pepTraces_raw <- annotateTraces(traces= traces_list,
                            trace_annotation= traceAnnotation,
                            traces_id_column = "protein_id",
                            trace_annotation_id_column = "input_id",
                            #trace_annotation_mass_column = "Mass",
                            uniprot_mass_format = TRUE,
                            replace_whitespace = TRUE)

#' # Annotate traces with molecular weight calibration
pepTraces_raw <- annotateMolecularWeight(pepTraces_raw,
                                     calibration)

summary(pepTraces_raw)
```


# 5. QC with CCprofiler

## 5a. alignment

```{r align across all samples}

#' # Assess how well SEC traces align across all samples
alignTraces(pepTraces_raw, min_lag = -3, max_lag = 3, plot = T, PDF=F) #to save as PDF, set PDF=T
#' # Assess total intensity as a proxi for extraction efficiency across samples
plotGlobalIntensities(pepTraces_raw, plot = T, PDF=F)
```

## 5b. annotateOutlierFractions & impute

```{r}
if(run_annotateOutlierFractions == T) {
pepTracesOutlFrac <- annotateOutlierFractions(traces=pepTraces_raw,
                                              excludeBoarder = T,
                                              plot = F)

annotateOutlierFractions(traces=pepTraces_raw,
                         excludeBoarder = T,
                         plot = TRUE,
                         PDF = FALSE,
                         name = "OutlierFractions")
}
```

```{r}

if(run_annotateOutlierFractions == F) {
pepTracesOutlFrac <- lapply(pepTraces_raw, function(lst) {
  # Add the isOutlier column to the fraction_annotation data frame
    # Check if 'filename' is in problematic_fractions and create the isOutlier column
  lst$fraction_annotation$isOutlier <- lst$fraction_annotation$filename %in% problematic_fractions
  
  return(lst)
})

# Reassign the class to "tracesList"
class(pepTracesOutlFrac) <- class(pepTraces_raw)

# Check the class of the output
class(pepTracesOutlFrac)
}
  
```

```{r}
### NB NB NB, use mean, not spline, mean is much better, Benni said :) But there is a bug in the code -> even if you set 'mean', it will go for spline anyway -> fix the bug (Benni already did...)

pepTracesOutlFracImp <- imputeOutlierFractions( traces = pepTracesOutlFrac,
                                                bound_left = 2,
                                                bound_right = 2,
                                                consider_borders = TRUE,
                                                method = "spline" # mean is also an option
)
```

## 5c. general imputation

```{r Find missing values}

  
#' Find missing values
#' (defined as having identifications in left and right neigbouring fractions):
pepTracesMV <- findMissingValues(pepTracesOutlFracImp,
                                 bound_left = 1,
                                 bound_right = 1,
                                 consider_borders = TRUE)

#' Impute NA values by fitting a spline:
pepTracesImp <- imputeMissingVals(pepTracesMV, method = "spline")

#' Plot imputation summary:
plotImputationSummary(pepTracesMV, pepTracesImp, PDF = F,
                      plot_traces = T, max_n_traces = 2)

```



## 5d. normalization

```{r Normalize intensity values across samples}

if(perform_normalization_cyclicloess==T) {
# Normalize intensity values across samples
pepTracesNormalized <- normalizeByCyclicLoess(pepTracesImp, window = 3, step = 1, plot = F) 

normalizeByCyclicLoess(pepTracesImp, window = 3, step = 1, plot = T, PDF = F) #, name = "normalizeByCyclicLoess")

#' # Assess total intensity after normalization
plotGlobalIntensities(pepTracesNormalized, plot = T, PDF=F ) #, name = "IntensitySummary_postNormalization")
}


if(perform_normalization_cyclicloess==F) { pepTracesNormalized <- pepTracesImp}

```

### -Trypsin plot for QC before  and after normalization
```{r}
plot(subset(pepTraces_raw, trace_subset_ids="CON_P00761", trace_subset_type="protein_id"), legend=F, design_matrix=design_matrix, name="Trypsin non-normalized")
plot(subset(pepTracesNormalized, trace_subset_ids="CON_P00761", trace_subset_type="protein_id"), legend=F, design_matrix=design_matrix, name="Trypsin normalized")
```


### - Calculate the ratios between trypsin peptides for each fraction
```{r}
trypsin_data <- as.data.frame(integrateTraceIntensities(subset(pepTraces_raw, trace_subset_ids="CON_P00761", trace_subset_type="protein_id"))$traces[,1:32])

trypsin_peptide_averages <- rowSums(trypsin_data)/32

trypsin_ratios <- trypsin_data

for(i in 1:nrow(trypsin_ratios)){
  trypsin_ratios[i,] <- trypsin_data[i,]/trypsin_peptide_averages[i]
}

trypsin_ratios[trypsin_ratios==0] <- NA

median_trypsin_ratio <- double(length=ncol(trypsin_ratios))
for(j in 1:ncol(trypsin_ratios)){
  median_trypsin_ratio[j] <- median(trypsin_ratios[sort(trypsin_peptide_averages, index.return=T, decreasing=T)$ix[1:5], j], na.rm=T)
}
pepTraces_trypsinNormalized <- pepTracesImp
for(experiment_id in names(pepTraces_trypsinNormalized)){
  for(i in 1:32){
    i_str <- as.character(i)
    pepTraces_trypsinNormalized[[experiment_id]]$traces[,(i_str):=pepTraces_trypsinNormalized[[experiment_id]]$traces[,i:i]/median_trypsin_ratio[i]]
  }
}
```


## - Pep-lvl filtering

```{r}
#' # Filter by consecutive IDs --> comment out: and peptide correlation--> work with pepTracesConsIds instead of pepTracesSPC
#' 
library(CCprofilerDiffAnna)

# Combine all traces for filtering:

traces_combined <- combineTracesMutiCond(pepTracesNormalized)
# Filter by consecutive IDs:
pepTracesConsIds <- filterConsecutiveIdStretches(traces_combined,
                                                 min_stretch_length = 3,
                                                 remove_empty = T)

# Filter by maximum correlation for outlier removal:
pepTracesMaxCorr <- filterByMaxCorr(pepTracesConsIds,
                                cutoff = 0.8,
                                plot = T, PDF = F)

# Filter by consecutive SPC:
# pepTracesSPC <- filterBySibPepCorr(pepTracesMaxCorr,
#                                    absolute_spcCutoff = 0.6,
#                                    plot = T, PDF = F)
```

```{r}
#' # Subset pepTracesNormalized to valid peptides selected 
#' by consecutive filtering and SPC cutoff
validPeps <- unique(pepTracesMaxCorr$trace_annotation$id)
pepTracesList_filtered <- lapply(pepTracesNormalized, function(x){
  sub_trace <- subset(x, trace_subset_ids=validPeps)
  sub_trace$trace_annotation <- sub_trace$trace_annotation# %>% #left_join(select(pepTracesMaxCorr$trace_annotation, id #SibPepCorr), by=join_by(id)) # Inserts the sibling peptide correlation data
 sub_trace
})
class(pepTracesList_filtered) <- "tracesList"

#' Update traces with additional metrics for each fraction:
pepTracesList_filtered <- updateTraces(pepTracesList_filtered)

#' Inspect traces list:
summary(pepTracesList_filtered)

```



## - Global stats

```{r}
#' # Global stats
traces_names <- c(
  "pepTraces_raw",
  "pepTracesList_filtered"
)

countDT <- data.table(evidence=character(), sample=character(), count=integer(), traces=character())

for (traces in traces_names) {
  #traces <- readRDS(paste0(traces_name,".rda"))
  traces_name <- traces
  traces <- eval(as.name(traces))

  getIDs <- lapply(traces, function(x){
    peptides <- unique(x$trace_annotation[grep("DECOY",protein_id, invert = T)]$id)
    proteins <- unique(x$trace_annotation[grep("DECOY",protein_id, invert = T)]$protein_id)
    list(peptides=length(peptides),proteins=length(proteins))
  })

  count_dt <- as.data.table(getIDs)
  count_dt[, evidence := c("peptides", "proteins")]
  count_dt <- melt(count_dt, id.vars = "evidence", variable.name = "sample", value.name = "count")
  count_dt[,traces:=traces_name]
  count_dt[, count := unlist(count)]
  countDT <- rbind(countDT,count_dt)
}

traces_names_dt <- data.table(
  traces=c("pepTraces_raw",
           "pepTracesList_filtered"),
  traces_name=c("raw",
                "filtered")
)

countDT <- merge(countDT, traces_names_dt, by="traces")

countDT$traces_name <- factor(countDT$traces_name, levels=traces_names_dt$traces_name)

# write.table(countDT, "traces_stat_counts.txt", sep="t",quote = F, col.names = T, row.names = F)
# 
# pdf("traces_stat_counts.pdf",height=9,width=12)

g <- ggplot(countDT,aes(x=traces_name,y=count,fill=evidence, group=sample)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity") +
     geom_label(label = countDT$count) +
  facet_wrap(sample ~ evidence, scales="free",nrow=3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9"))+
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position="bottom") + theme(legend.title = element_blank()) +
  labs(y = "count") +
  theme(axis.title.x=element_blank()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)) 

print(g)


```

# 6. Protein quantification

# 6.-1. ELUTION AWARE ANALYSIS (maxLFQ and peptide level)
```{r}
# Source all modules
source("./ELUTION_AWARE/CFMS_Elution_Pipeline_Protein.R")
source("./ELUTION_AWARE/CFMS_Elution_Pipeline_Peptide.R")
source("./ELUTION_AWARE/CFMS_Elution_Pipeline_Comparison.R")
source("./ELUTION_AWARE/CFMS_Elution_Pipeline_QC.R")

# Pipeline 3: Protein-level
results_p3 <- run_protein_elution_pipeline(
  traces_list = pepTracesList_filtered,
  design_matrix = NULL,
  ref_condition = "ctrl",
  treat_condition = "aSYN",
  output_dir = "./ELUTION_AWARE/pipeline3_results",
  gaussian_params = list(
    max_gaussians = 3, #default5
    min_R_squared = 0.3, # default 0.5
    min_consecutive = 5,
    min_iterations = 5, # increasable to 5 for more precise results (slower though)
    max_iterations = 10, # default 50 (very slow!)
    filter_gaussians_center = TRUE,
    filter_gaussians_height = 0.15,
    filter_gaussians_variance_min = 0.5,
    filter_gaussians_variance_max = 50,
    filter_gaussians_min_dist = 1
  )
)

# Pipeline 4: Peptide-level (both approaches)
results_p4 <- run_peptide_elution_pipeline(
  traces_list = pepTracesList_filtered,
  design_matrix = NULL,
  ref_condition = "ctrl",
  treat_condition = "aSYN",
  output_dir = "./ELUTION_AWARE/pipeline4_results",
  gaussian_params = list(
    max_gaussians = 3,
    min_R_squared = 0.3,
    min_consecutive = 5,
    min_iterations = 5,
    max_iterations = 10,
    filter_gaussians_center = TRUE,
    filter_gaussians_height = 0.15,
    filter_gaussians_variance_min = 0.5,
    filter_gaussians_variance_max = 50,
    filter_gaussians_min_dist = 1
  )
)


# Results in: pipeline4_results/adaptive_aggregation/
#             pipeline4_results/proteoform_aware/

# run QC
qc_p3 <- run_comprehensive_qc(results_p3, pipeline_type = "protein",
                               output_dir = "./ELUTION_AWARE/pipeline3_qc")
qc_p4 <- run_comprehensive_qc(results_p4, pipeline_type = "peptide",
                               output_dir = "./ELUTION_AWARE/pipeline4_qc")

# run Comparison
comparison <- compare_elution_pipelines(
  pipeline3_results = results_p3,
  pipeline4_results = results_p4,
  pval_threshold = 0.1,
  output_dir = "./ELUTION_AWARE/pipeline_comparison"
)

##########################################
source("./ELUTION_AWARE/CFMS_Elution_Pipeline_P3_Height_UPGRADED_CUSTOMIDS_ONLY_NAimpute.R") # 4 main steps so it is closer to the gaussian piepline of Aljaz

results_height <- run_p3_height_pipeline(
  traces_list = pepTracesList_filtered,
  ref_condition = "ctrl",
  treat_condition = "aSYN",
  
  # Proteoform clustering (SAME as P3)
  distance_threshold = 1.0,
  min_peptides = 3,
  min_peptides_maxlfq = 2,
  
  # Peak detection (NEW!)
  peak_params = list(
    min_height_fraction = 0.10,
    min_prominence = 0.15,
    min_distance = 3
  ),
   quant_window = 5,                       # Quantification cap: integration window is adaptive now, but never wider than ±5 fractions.
                                          # Think of this as a safety cap against integrating too broadly.

  # --- Peak overlap / ownership handling (NEW) ---
  peak_ownership = "soft",                # "hard" or "soft"
                                          # hard: midpoint boundaries between peak centers (fast; deterministic; no mixing within boundaries)
                                          # soft: proxy shapes → per-fraction ownership; fractions kept only if ownership ≥ min_peak_fraction
  min_peak_fraction = 0.5,                # Only used when peak_ownership="soft".
                                          # 0.5 = keep fractions where this peak contributes at least half (like your Gaussian logic).
                                          # Raise (0.6–0.8) for stricter de-mixing; lower (0.3–0.4) if peaks overlap heavily.

  # --- Peptide support QC in the peak region (NEW) ---
  min_npep = 5,                           # Minimum number of peptides that show signal (>0) in the final included peak fractions.
                                          # Increase to be stricter; decrease if you have low-coverage proteins.
  min_weighted_corr = 0.2,                # Minimum intensity-weighted peptide correlation within the peak region.
                                          # Helps drop “peaks” driven by inconsistent peptides/noise.
                                          # Typical: 0.2 mild, 0.4 moderate, 0.6 strict.

  # --- Peak matching across conditions (shift-aware) ---
  position_tolerance = 3,                 # When quantifying a reference peak in a sample, search ±3 fractions around the reference center
                                          # to find the local max (shift handling).
                                          # Larger = tolerates shifts but risks matching the wrong nearby peak.
                                          # Smaller = safer matching but can miss shifted peaks.

  detect_new_peaks = TRUE,                # If TRUE, also find peaks present in treatment that are NOT near any ctrl reference peak.
                                          # NEW peaks are detected per treatment replicate, then clustered across treatment replicates only
                                          # (stable IDs), then quantified in all samples.

  min_width_estimate = 2,                 # Width QC: minimum estimated halfwidth (FWHM-ish) for a detected peak.
                                          # 1 = basically no filtering (flag only)
                                          # 2 = mild filtering (drops razor-thin spikes)
                                          # 3 = stricter QC (recommended if you see lots of spurious single-fraction peaks)

  
  n_trace_plots = 20,  # Number of significant proteoforms to plot
  custom_ids = c("P06611_A", "P06611_B","P06611_C", "P37028_A", "P06609_A", "P0A6F5_A", "P0A7E5_A", "P0A8G0_A"), #BtuD, BtuF, BtuC (Entabolon shift) and GroEL (sticky contaminant), and CTP synthase and UvrABC (positives controls for ATP dataset)

  return_peak_support_tables = TRUE,       # NEW: writes/returns peak support summaries (e.g., 2/3 replicates detected).
 
  
  output_dir = "p3_height_results_NAimpute"
)


################PROTEIN LEVEL NO CLUSTERING##############
source("./ELUTION_AWARE/CFMS_Elution_Pipeline_P3_Height_PROTEIN_LEVEL_FIXED.R")

# MaxLFQ (default) - more accurate, normalizes across samples
results_median <- run_protein_level_pipeline(
  traces_list = pepTracesList_filtered_label, # or _light or _heavy, (use filtered with SibCorr&CO)
  ref_condition = "GFP",
  treat_condition = "intox",
  aggregation_method = "median", # or "maxlfq", "median", "sum", "top3", "top5", "top10"
  pvalue_adjustment = "qvalue",
  n_permutations = 0,        # 100-1000 (explöoratory: 100, ~10 min running; publication: 1000, ~1h running. with n=3 does not work!)
  adaptive_window = TRUE,      # ← Enable adaptive (FALSE, fixed "quant_window=5", means +/- 5 fractions around peak center)
  width_multiplier = 1.5,      # ← 1.5 × FWHM (Full Width at Half Maximum)
  custom_ids = c("Q04781", "Q05468", "Q12532", "P25694", "P53044", "P33755", "Q04311", "P37840", "P42212"), # ribosome quality control complex and associated proteins and aSYn and GFP at the end.
  output_dir = "./ELUTION_AWARE/protein_results_median_10h"
)
# Source the plotting script
source("./ELUTION_AWARE/plot_protein_traces.R")
# 1. Custom proteins
plot_protein_traces(results_median, protein_ids = c("P42212","P37840",
#                                                         "P10591", "P15108", "P15992", "P25294", "P29311", "P38903", "P04840", "P07251", "P23287", "P25296", "P31109", "P05030", "P35180", "P07170", "Q04432", "P16474", "P36108", "P01123", "P07560", "Q99260", "Q12697"), # claude AI aSYN interactors
"P31109", "P33328", "P32867", "P39926", "P40357", "P20606", "P15303", "P40482", "	
P01123", "Q04491", "P38968", "P40335", "P38759", "P34110", "P49334", "P07251", "P00830","P22141", "P30656", "P23724", "P30657", "P23639", "P38624", "P23638", "P21242", "P40303", "P40302", "P43588", "P38886", "Q06103", "P38764", "P33297", "P40327", "P40016", "Q04062", "P32565", "P33298", "P33299", "Q12250", "Q01939", "Q08723","P06787", "P53968", "P29311", "P34730", "P01123", "P43588"),  # complex disturbance by aSYN claudeAI                 
 output_dir = "./ELUTION_AWARE/protein_results_median_10h/costum_plots/claudeAI_complexes_disturbed")

# 2. Significant proteins
plot_significant_proteins(results, max_plots = 30)

# 3. High fold-change (|log2FC| > 2)
plot_high_fc_proteins(results, fc_threshold = 2, max_plots = 30)

# 4. All interesting proteins (significant + high FC)
plot_interesting_proteins(
  results,
  pval_threshold = 0.05,
  fc_threshold_sig = 1,
  fc_threshold_high = 2,
  max_plots = 50
)

# 5. Top 20 by p-value
plot_top_proteins(results_top10median, criterion = "pvalue", n = 20, output_dir = "./ELUTION_AWARE/protein_results_top10median_10h/plots_top_by_p-val")



##########proteoform traces plotting###############
source("./ELUTION_AWARE/plot_proteoform_peptide_traces.R")

# Plot the best-clustered proteoforms
# quality_metrics <- plot_best_proteoform_peptides(
#   traces_list = pepTracesList_filtered,
#   pipeline_results = results_height,
#   n_top = 10,                                    # Top 10 best-clustered
#   custom_proteoforms = c("P06611_A", "P0A6F5_A", "P0A6T5_A", "	P0A8X2_A"), # Optional: also plot these
#   output_dir = "peptide_trace_plots",
#   file_format = "both"                           # PDF and PNG
# )

# Option A: Best clustering quality (sibling peptide correlation)
plot_by_correlation(pepTracesList_filtered, results_height, n_top = 10)

# Option B: Most significant p-value
plot_by_pvalue(pepTracesList_filtered, results_height, n_top = 10)

# Option C: Largest fold change
plot_by_foldchange(pepTracesList_filtered, results_height, n_top = 10)

# Option D: Custom selection
plot_custom(pepTracesList_filtered, results, 
            custom_ids = c("P75966", "P33634", "P0AFK4", "P0A9X9", "P09323"),
             output_dir = "./ELUTION_AWARE/peptide_trace_plots_median")


# 4. Explore the quality metrics
head(quality_metrics)
high_quality <- quality_metrics %>% filter(mean_sib_corr > 0.8)

#DIAGNOSTIC for protein aggregation method
source("./ELUTION_AWARE/diagnose_aggregation.R")
diagnose_aggregation_sparsity(
  traces_list = pepTracesList_filtered_label,
  protein_map = results_median$protein_map,
  proteins    = results_median$proteins,
  custom_ids  = c("Q04781", "Q05468", "Q12532", "P25694", "P53044",
                   "P33755", "Q04311", "P37840", "P42212")
)



```



```{r}

#' ## Quantify on protein level
# MODIFIED CODE with maxLFQ:
# Your existing code up to Phase 4...
# pepTracesList_filtered is ready

# Load the hybrid module
source("CFMS_Hybrid_Pipeline_Module.R")
source("CFMS_Pipeline_Integration.R")

# Run the complete integrated analysis
results <- run_integrated_analysis(
  pepTracesList_filtered = pepTracesList_filtered,
  design_matrix = design_matrix,
  ref_condition = "ctrl",
  treat_conditions = c("aSYN"),
  output_dir = "hybrid_analysis"
)

# Access results
maxlfq_data <- results$hybrid$maxlfq
proteoform_info <- results$hybrid$piqed
differential <- results$hybrid$differential_combined
predicted_complexes <- results$complexes$complexes$assignments

# Get significant hits (with proteoform info)
significant <- get_significant_hits(differential, padj_threshold = 0.05)


# Source the module (after running hybrid pipeline)
source("CFMS_Comparison_Module.R")

# Run the comparison
comparison <- compare_maxlfq_vs_proteoform(
  combined_results = results$hybrid$differential_combined,
  piqed_results = results$hybrid$piqed,
  concordance_threshold = 0.5,
  output_dir = "comparison_output",
  save_plots = TRUE
)


# Access results
comparison$summary_stats        # Summary statistics
comparison$hidden_hits          # Proteins significant ONLY in proteoform analysis
comparison$discordant_proteins  # All discordant proteins
comparison$recommendations      # Which method to use for each protein

# Create final "best" results table
final_results <- create_recommended_results(
  results$hybrid$differential_combined,
  comparison
)
      
```
# 6a. visualize best candidates and PIQED first analysis v2

```{r}
source("CFMS_PIQED_First_Pipeline.R")

# Visualize top candidates from your current analysis
top_viz <- visualize_top_candidates(
  combined_results = results$hybrid$differential_combined,
  n_top = 50,
  raw_pval_threshold = 0.05,
  output_dir = "top_candidates_viz"
)

# See summary
top_viz$summary

# See top candidates table
head(top_viz$top_candidates, 20)

########## Run the NEW pipeline with PIQED first, MaxLFQ on consistent peptides only###############
results_v2 <- run_piqed_first_pipeline(
  traces_list = pepTracesList_filtered,
  design_matrix = design_matrix,
  ref_condition = "ctrl",
  treat_conditions = c("aSYN"),
  distance_threshold = 1.0,
  min_peptides_clustering = 3,
  use_ibmt = TRUE,
  output_dir = "piqed_first_results"
)

# Compare results
results_v2$differential_combined

# Old results
old_n_sig <- sum(results$hybrid$differential_combined$pBHadj_aSYN < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
old_min_p <- min(results$hybrid$differential_combined$pBHadj_aSYN, na.rm = TRUE)

# New results
new_n_sig <- sum(results_v2$differential_combined$pBHadj_aSYN < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
new_min_p <- min(results_v2$differential_combined$pBHadj_aSYN, na.rm = TRUE)

message(paste("OLD: Significant proteins:", old_n_sig, "| Min adj.p:", old_min_p))
message(paste("NEW: Significant proteins:", new_n_sig, "| Min adj.p:", new_min_p))

# 1. Visualize PIQED-first results (same function, new data)
top_viz_v2 <- visualize_top_candidates(
  combined_results = results_v2$differential_combined,
  n_top = 50,
  raw_pval_threshold = 0.05,
  output_dir = "top_candidates_piqed_first"
)

# 2. Compare OLD vs PIQED-first pipelines (NEW FUNCTION!)
comparison <- compare_old_vs_piqed_first(
  old_results = results$hybrid$differential_combined,
  new_results = results_v2$differential_combined,
  output_dir = "pipeline_comparison"
)

# 3. View the comparison summary
comparison$old_summary
comparison$new_summary

# 4. See which proteins improved the most
head(comparison$matched_proteins[order(new_pval)], 20)

###### Check improvement specifically for flagged proteins
# Check this output:
comparison$old_summary
comparison$new_summary

flagged_comparison <- comparison$matched_proteins[is_flagged == TRUE]
cat("Flagged proteins - P-value improved:", sum(flagged_comparison$pval_improved, na.rm=TRUE), 
    "out of", nrow(flagged_comparison), "\n")


####################hybrid pipeline
# Source the new module
source("CFMS_Hybrid_Best_Pipeline.R")

# Run the hybrid best-of-both-worlds pipeline
results_hybrid <- run_hybrid_best_pipeline(
  traces_list = pepTracesList_filtered,
  design_matrix = design_matrix,
  ref_condition = "ctrl",
  treat_conditions = c("aSYN"),
  distance_threshold = 1.0,
  min_peptides_clustering = 3,
  use_ibmt = TRUE,
  output_dir = "hybrid_best_results"
)

# Visualize results
top_viz_hybrid <- visualize_top_candidates(
  combined_results = results_hybrid$differential_combined,
  n_top = 50,
  output_dir = "top_candidates_hybrid"
)

```

# 6b. fix BH p-value adjustment
```{r}
# ============================================================
# QUICK FIX: Recalculate BH correction on MAIN analysis only
# ============================================================

# Get main analysis results only (one per protein)
main_only <- results_hybrid$differential_combined[analysis_type == "Hybrid_main"]

# Recalculate BH correction on main analysis only
main_only[, pBHadj_aSYN := p.adjust(pval_aSYN, method = "BH")]

# Check the improvement
cat("=== CORRECTED STATISTICS ===\n")
cat("Total proteins (main analysis):", nrow(main_only), "\n")
cat("Adj.p < 0.05:", sum(main_only$pBHadj_aSYN < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Adj.p < 0.10:", sum(main_only$pBHadj_aSYN < 0.10, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Raw p < 0.05:", sum(main_only$pval_aSYN < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Min adj.p:", min(main_only$pBHadj_aSYN, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

# Check for p=1 problem
cat("\n=== DIAGNOSTIC ===\n")
cat("Proteins with p-value = 1.0:", sum(main_only$pval_aSYN == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Proteins with p-value = NA:", sum(is.na(main_only$pval_aSYN)), "\n")

# Visualize corrected distribution
hist(main_only$pBHadj_aSYN, breaks = 50, 
     main = "CORRECTED: Adjusted P-value Distribution",
     xlab = "Adjusted P-value (BH on main analysis only)")
abline(v = 0.05, col = "red", lty = 2)

# How many proteins have p-value = 1?
pval_1_proteins <- main_only[pval_aSYN == 1]
cat("Proteins with p=1:", nrow(pval_1_proteins), "\n")

# What do they look like?
head(pval_1_proteins[, .(protein_id, pval_aSYN, log2FC_aSYN, n_peptides_total, is_flagged)])

# Check if they have 0 variance
# This often happens when all replicates have the same value


# Check RAW p-value distribution (this is more informative)
hist(main_only$pval_aSYN, breaks = 50, 
     main = "RAW P-value Distribution", 
     xlab = "Raw P-value")
abline(v = 0.05, col = "red", lty = 2)

# Count adjusted p-values = 1
cat("Adjusted p = 1.0:", sum(main_only$pBHadj_aSYN >= 0.99, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Adjusted p < 1.0:", sum(main_only$pBHadj_aSYN < 0.99, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

```

# 6c. peptide level analysis ALL peptides
```{r}
# Source the module
source("CFMS_Peptide_Level_Analysis.R")

# Run peptide-level analysis (like PIQED)
results_peptide <- run_peptide_level_analysis(
  traces_list = pepTracesList_filtered,
  design_matrix = design_matrix,
  ref_condition = "ctrl",
  treat_conditions = c("aSYN"),
  min_valid_samples = 4,
  use_ibmt = TRUE,
  output_dir = "peptide_level_results"
)

# Compare all FOUR pipelines
comparison_all <- compare_all_four_pipelines(
  old_results = results$hybrid$differential_combined,
  piqed_first_results = results_v2$differential_combined,
  hybrid_results = results_hybrid$differential_combined,
  peptide_results = results_peptide,
  output_dir = "four_pipeline_comparison"
)
```


# 6d. RAW p-value distribution comparisons
```{r}
# ============================================================
# RAW P-VALUE DISTRIBUTION COMPARISON: ALL THREE PIPELINES
# ============================================================

library(ggplot2)
library(data.table)

# --- Extract MAIN analysis results from each pipeline ---

# OLD pipeline (MaxLFQ-first)
old_main <- results$hybrid$differential_combined[analysis_type == "MaxLFQ_main"]
old_pvals <- data.table(
  pval = old_main$pval_aSYN,
  pipeline = "1. OLD (MaxLFQ-first)"
)

# PIQED-first pipeline
# Check what analysis_type is called in your results_v2
piqed_main <- results_v2$differential_combined[grepl("MaxLFQ|main", analysis_type, ignore.case = TRUE)]
# If that doesn't work, try:
# piqed_main <- results_v2$differential_combined[grepl("_A$", proteoform_id)]
piqed_pvals <- data.table(
  pval = piqed_main$pval_aSYN,
  pipeline = "2. PIQED-first"
)

# HYBRID pipeline
hybrid_main <- results_hybrid$differential_combined[analysis_type == "Hybrid_main"]
hybrid_pvals <- data.table(
  pval = hybrid_main$pval_aSYN,
  pipeline = "3. HYBRID"
)

# Combine all
all_pvals <- rbind(old_pvals, piqed_pvals, hybrid_pvals)

# --- Print Summary Statistics ---
cat("\n============================================================\n")
cat("         RAW P-VALUE COMPARISON: ALL THREE PIPELINES        \n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")

summary_stats <- all_pvals[, .(
  n_proteins = .N,
  n_raw_p01 = sum(pval < 0.01, na.rm = TRUE),
  n_raw_p05 = sum(pval < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
  min_pval = min(pval, na.rm = TRUE),
  median_pval = median(pval, na.rm = TRUE),
  mean_pval = mean(pval, na.rm = TRUE)
), by = pipeline]

print(summary_stats)

# --- Plot 1: Overlaid Histograms ---
p1 <- ggplot(all_pvals, aes(x = pval, fill = pipeline)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = 0.5, position = "identity") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("1. OLD (MaxLFQ-first)" = "steelblue", 
                                "2. PIQED-first" = "coral",
                                "3. HYBRID" = "forestgreen")) +
  labs(
    title = "Raw P-value Distribution Comparison",
    subtitle = "Overlaid histograms | Red line = p = 0.05",
    x = "Raw P-value",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Pipeline"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p1)

# --- Plot 2: Faceted Histograms (easier to see) ---
p2 <- ggplot(all_pvals, aes(x = pval, fill = pipeline)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, alpha = 0.7, color = "white") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("1. OLD (MaxLFQ-first)" = "steelblue", 
                                "2. PIQED-first" = "coral",
                                "3. HYBRID" = "forestgreen")) +
  facet_wrap(~pipeline, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Raw P-value Distribution by Pipeline",
    subtitle = "Red line = p = 0.05 | Enrichment near 0 indicates true effects",
    x = "Raw P-value",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

print(p2)

# --- Plot 3: Zoom into low p-values (where the action is) ---
p3 <- ggplot(all_pvals[pval < 0.2], aes(x = pval, fill = pipeline)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, alpha = 0.6, position = "identity") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.01, linetype = "dotted", color = "darkred", linewidth = 1) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("1. OLD (MaxLFQ-first)" = "steelblue", 
                                "2. PIQED-first" = "coral",
                                "3. HYBRID" = "forestgreen")) +
  labs(
    title = "Raw P-value Distribution (Zoomed: p < 0.2)",
    subtitle = "Red dashed = 0.05 | Dotted = 0.01",
    x = "Raw P-value",
    y = "Count",
    fill = "Pipeline"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

print(p3)

# --- Plot 4: Bar chart comparing significant counts ---
sig_counts <- all_pvals[, .(
  `p < 0.001` = sum(pval < 0.001, na.rm = TRUE),
  `p < 0.01` = sum(pval < 0.01, na.rm = TRUE),
  `p < 0.05` = sum(pval < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
  `p < 0.1` = sum(pval < 0.1, na.rm = TRUE)
), by = pipeline]

sig_counts_long <- melt(sig_counts, id.vars = "pipeline", 
                         variable.name = "threshold", value.name = "count")

p4 <- ggplot(sig_counts_long, aes(x = threshold, y = count, fill = pipeline)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = count), position = position_dodge(width = 0.7), 
            vjust = -0.5, size = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("1. OLD (MaxLFQ-first)" = "steelblue", 
                                "2. PIQED-first" = "coral",
                                "3. HYBRID" = "forestgreen")) +
  labs(
    title = "Number of Proteins by Raw P-value Threshold",
    x = "P-value Threshold",
    y = "Number of Proteins",
    fill = "Pipeline"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, max(sig_counts_long$count) * 1.15))

print(p4)

# --- Save plots ---
ggsave("pval_comparison_overlay.pdf", p1, width = 10, height = 6)
ggsave("pval_comparison_facet.pdf", p2, width = 10, height = 10)
ggsave("pval_comparison_zoomed.pdf", p3, width = 10, height = 6)
ggsave("pval_comparison_bars.pdf", p4, width = 10, height = 6)

cat("\nPlots saved!\n")
```

# 6e. check peptide level behaviour - opposite directions ect.
```{r}
# Check what columns exist in protein_summary
names(results_peptide$protein_summary)

# Get peptide-level results directly and calculate directions
peptide_results <- results_peptide$peptide_results

# Calculate direction consistency per protein
direction_summary <- peptide_results[, .(
  n_peptides = .N,
  n_positive_fc = sum(log2FC_aSYN > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
  n_negative_fc = sum(log2FC_aSYN < 0, na.rm = TRUE),
  n_sig_p05 = sum(pval_aSYN < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
  mean_fc = mean(log2FC_aSYN, na.rm = TRUE),
  fc_sd = sd(log2FC_aSYN, na.rm = TRUE),
  fc_range = max(log2FC_aSYN, na.rm = TRUE) - min(log2FC_aSYN, na.rm = TRUE),
  min_pval = min(pval_aSYN, na.rm = TRUE)
), by = protein_id]

# Calculate percentages and flags
direction_summary[, pct_positive := 100 * n_positive_fc / n_peptides]
direction_summary[, pct_negative := 100 * n_negative_fc / n_peptides]

# Flag proteins with mixed directions (both up and down peptides, at least 3 peptides)
direction_summary[, is_mixed := n_positive_fc > 0 & n_negative_fc > 0 & n_peptides >= 3]

# Flag highly inconsistent (large FC range)
direction_summary[, is_highly_inconsistent := fc_range > 1.5 & n_peptides >= 3]

# Summary
cat("\n=== PEPTIDE DIRECTION CONSISTENCY ===\n")
cat("Total proteins:", nrow(direction_summary), "\n")
cat("Proteins with MIXED peptide directions:", sum(direction_summary$is_mixed), "\n")
cat("Proteins with CONSISTENT directions:", sum(!direction_summary$is_mixed), "\n")
cat("Proteins highly inconsistent (FC range > 1.5):", sum(direction_summary$is_highly_inconsistent, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

# Among mixed-direction proteins, how many have significant peptides?
mixed_proteins <- direction_summary[is_mixed == TRUE]
cat("\nAmong MIXED proteins:\n")
cat("  With at least 1 sig peptide (p<0.05):", sum(mixed_proteins$n_sig_p05 > 0), "\n")
cat("  With at least 2 sig peptides:", sum(mixed_proteins$n_sig_p05 >= 2), "\n")

# Look at top mixed-direction proteins with significant peptides
cat("\n=== TOP MIXED-DIRECTION PROTEINS WITH SIGNIFICANT PEPTIDES ===\n")
mixed_with_sig <- direction_summary[is_mixed == TRUE & n_sig_p05 > 0]
setorder(mixed_with_sig, -n_sig_p05, min_pval)
print(head(mixed_with_sig[, .(protein_id, n_peptides, n_positive_fc, n_negative_fc, 
                               n_sig_p05, mean_fc, fc_range, min_pval)], 20))

# Visualize
library(ggplot2)

# Plot: Distribution of direction consistency
p1 <- ggplot(direction_summary, aes(x = pct_positive)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(25, 75), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Peptide Direction Consistency per Protein",
    subtitle = "0% = all peptides DOWN | 100% = all peptides UP | 50% = mixed",
    x = "% Peptides with Positive FC",
    y = "Number of Proteins"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p1)

# Plot: FC range vs significance
p2 <- ggplot(direction_summary[n_peptides >= 3], aes(x = fc_range, y = -log10(min_pval))) +
  geom_point(aes(color = is_mixed), alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "forestgreen", "TRUE" = "coral"),
                     labels = c("Consistent", "Mixed")) +
  labs(
    title = "Peptide FC Range vs Best P-value",
    subtitle = "Mixed-direction proteins may have opposing proteoform effects",
    x = "FC Range (max - min log2FC)",
    y = "-log10(min p-value)",
    color = "Direction"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p2)
```

# 6f. peptide.-level wioth proper prptein-aggregation (simes)
```{r}
# Re-source the updated module
source("CFMS_Peptide_Level_Analysis_2.R")

# 1. Aggregate peptide p-values to protein level
protein_from_peptides <- aggregate_peptides_to_protein(
  results_peptide, 
  method = "simes"  # Best for correlated peptides
)

# View top proteins
head(protein_from_peptides[, .(protein_id, n_peptides, pval_aggregated, 
                                pBHadj_aggregated, mean_fc, n_sig_peptides_p05)])

# 2. Compare ALL methods
method_comparison <- compare_aggregation_methods(results_peptide)

# 3. Full comparison including peptide→protein aggregation
comparison_all <- compare_all_four_pipelines(
  old_results = results$hybrid$differential_combined,
  piqed_first_results = results_v2$differential_combined,
  hybrid_results = results_hybrid$differential_combined,
  peptide_results = results_peptide,
  include_peptide_aggregated = TRUE,
  aggregation_method = "simes",
  output_dir = "five_pipeline_comparison"
)
```

# 6g.final proteoform aware peptide-level analysis

 What It Does (5 Steps)

| Step | What | Output |
|------|------|--------|
| **1** | Peptide-level differential analysis | `01_peptide_level_results.csv` |
| **2** | Simes aggregation to protein level | `02_protein_simes_aggregated.csv` |
| **3** | Identify bidirectional proteins | `03_bidirectional_proteins.csv` |
| **4** | Create final summary tables | `05_TOP_DISCOVERIES.csv`, `06_PROTEOFORM_CANDIDATES.csv` |
| **5** | Generate visualizations | `ALL_PLOTS.pdf` |


 Key Output Files
proteoform_aware_results/
├── 01_peptide_level_results.csv      ← All peptide p-values & FCs
├── 02_protein_simes_aggregated.csv   ← Protein p-values (Simes method)
├── 03_bidirectional_proteins.csv     ← Proteins with UP + DOWN peptides
├── 04_protein_direction_summary.csv  ← Direction stats per protein
├── 05_TOP_DISCOVERIES.csv            ← ⭐ Best hits by Simes p-value
├── 06_PROTEOFORM_CANDIDATES.csv      ← ⭐ Bidirectional = proteoform candidates!
├── ALL_PLOTS.pdf                     ← All visualizations
└── plot_*.pdf                        ← Individual plots

```{r}
# Source the FIXED module
source("CFMS_Proteoform_Aware_Final_v2.R")
source("CFMS_Top20_Peptide_Plots.R")

# Run with parallelization
results_final <- run_proteoform_aware_analysis(
  traces_list = pepTracesList_filtered,
  design_matrix = design_matrix,
  ref_condition = "ctrl",
  treat_conditions = c("aSYN"),
  min_valid_samples = 4,
  fc_threshold = 0.5,
  pval_threshold = 0.05,
  #n_cores = 4,  # Adjust based on your CPU (or leave NULL for auto-detect)
  output_dir = "proteoform_aware_results")

# Save PDF plots for top 20 candidates
save_top20_proteoform_plots(results_final, output_dir = "top20_peptide_plots")

# View top proteoform candidates
head(results_final$final_summary$proteoform_candidates, 20)

# View top discoveries
head(results_final$final_summary$top_discoveries, 20)

# See all peptides for a protein of interest
visualize_protein_peptides(results_final$peptide_results, "P0AFL6")

# # Your top proteins - look up each on UniProt
# top_proteins <- c("P0AFL6", "P0A9Q7", "P13029", "P22259", "P0AFI2",
#                   "P0A9M0", "P0ADG7", "P62768", "P52647", "P0A698",
#                   "P15038", "P0A786", "P0A8M6", "P05793", "P15977",
#                   "P33570", "P0A6K6", "P68767", "P0A6F3", "P00370")
# 
# # Quick lookup URL generator
# for (p in top_proteins) {
#   cat(paste0("https://www.uniprot.org/uniprotkb/", p, "/entry\n"))
# }


```


 What the Bidirectional Score Means

Bidirectional Score = (min of n_up_sig, n_down_sig) × 2 
                    + FC_range (capped at 3)
                    + -log10(best_pval)

Higher score = More confident proteoform-specific regulation
Example interpretation:
Score > 5: Strong evidence for distinct proteoforms responding differently
Score 3-5: Moderate evidence
Score < 3: Weak evidence


# 6h. aggregation analysis simes vs stouffer (peptide independencies)
```{r}
source("CFMS_Aggregation_Method_Analysis_v2.R")

agg_results <- run_aggregation_analysis(
  peptide_results = results_final$peptide_results,
  expression_matrix = results_final$expression_matrix,  # optional but recommended
  traces_list = pepTracesList_filtered,                 # for sibling correlation
  output_dir = "aggregation_analysis"
)

# See recommendation
agg_results$recommendation

# Get protein p-values with best method
best_pvals <- agg_results$method_comparison$merged_results
```

# 6i. Consistency analysis btw replicates vs within proteins
```{r}
source("CFMS_Replicate_Consistency_Analysis.R")

rep_results <- run_replicate_consistency_analysis(
  traces_list = pepTracesList_filtered,
  design_matrix = design_matrix,
  ref_condition = "ctrl",
  treat_condition = "aSYN",
  output_dir = "replicate_consistency_analysis"
)

# Key summary metrics
rep_results$summary

# Proteins with reproducible bidirectional behavior
repro_bidir <- rep_results$bidirectional[is_reproducibly_bidirectional == TRUE]
```


# 7. check with Ludovic code for peptide traces along chromatogram
```{r}
source("CFMS_Peptide_Chromatogram_Plots.R")

# Plot control samples with mean ± SD error bars
plots <- generate_chromatogram_plots(
  data = data,                        # Your data already in memory!
  condition_filter = "aSYN",          # Only control samples
  top_n_proteins = 20,                # Top 20 proteins
  top_n_peptides = 20,                # Top 20 peptides per protein
  plot_style = "mean_sd",             # Options: "mean_sd", "replicates", "combined"
  output_dir = "chromatogram_plots_aSYN",
  n_fractions = 32
)


source("CFMS_Peptide_Chromatogram_Plots_treat_vs_ctrl.R")

# ctrl vs aSYN side-by-side, top 100 proteins
plots_2 <- generate_comparison_plots(
  data = data,
  condition1 = "ctrl",
  condition2 = "aSYN",
  top_n_proteins = 100,
  top_n_peptides = 20,
  output_dir = "ctrl_vs_aSYN_plots",
  n_fractions = 32
)

my_proteins <- c("P06612", "P00579", "P22106", "P62399", "P09373", 
                 "P0A6C8", "P0A8V2", "P00864", "P0A8T7", "P21170", 
                 "P42641", "P06959", "P68767", "P23003", "P09831", 
                 "P0A6Z3", "P03018", "P0ABH9", "P0AFP6", "P25665")

plots_3 <- generate_comparison_plots(
  data = data,
  condition1 = "ctrl",
  condition2 = "aSYN",
  priority_proteins = my_proteins,
  top_n_proteins = 200,
  show_protein_sum = FALSE,   # Only colored peptide lines
  output_dir = "ctrl_vs_aSYN_peptides_only"
)

source("CFMS_Peptide_Chromatogram_Plots_zoom.R")

plots_4 <- generate_comparison_plots(
  data = data,
  condition1 = "ctrl",
  condition2 = "aSYN",
  priority_proteins = my_proteins,
  top_n_proteins = 100,
  auto_zoom = TRUE,                   # Default: ON
  intensity_threshold = 5000,         # Default: 5000
  show_protein_sum = FALSE,
  output_dir = "ctrl_vs_PEP_zoomed"
)

```


# GLOBAL assembly state

## - Protein Mass distribution

```{r}

# evaluate protein mass distribution
protTraces_assembly <- annotateMassDistribution(protein_traces_list)


uniqueProteins <- Reduce(intersect, lapply(protTraces_assembly, function(x){x$trace_annotation$id}))

protTraces_assembly <- subset(protTraces_assembly, trace_subset_ids = uniqueProteins)

lapply(names(protTraces_assembly), function(x){
  summarizeMassDistribution(protTraces_assembly[[x]], 
                            PDF=F) #,
                            #name=paste0(here("output_files/massDistribution_",x)))
  })

```

## - Get assembly changes

```{r}
n_conditions <- length(unique(design_matrix$Condition))

#create a list of vectors to save the all_proteins_independentOfAssemblyState; proteins_noAssemblyChanges; more_assembled_proteins; less_assembled_proteins; proteins_withAssemblyChanges

all_proteins_independentOfAssemblyState_list <- list()
proteins_noAssemblyChange_list <- list()
more_assembled_proteins_list <- list()
less_assembled_proteins_list <- list()
proteins_withAssemblyChanges_list <- list()
  

# compare the treatmets to the ref (the list is specified in the vectors treatment_conditions and ref_condition in the Analysis SETUP section)

diffAssemblyState <- getMassAssemblyChange_aljazfix(tracesList=protTraces_assembly,    
                                             design_matrix= design_matrix, 
                                             compare_between = "Condition",
                                             quantLevel = "protein_id",
                                             plot=T, 
                                             PDF=F #,
                                             #name = paste0("beta_pvalue_histogram_"ref_condition,"_",i)
                                             )

```

## - look-up traces of proteins WITH global assembly changes

LET'S have a look at these proteins that change in assembly in at least
one of the treatments:

```{r}
 # lookup all the proteins_withAssembly Changes in any of the treatment conditions


for(i in filter(diffAssemblyState, betaPval_BHadj < 0.05 & abs(meanDiff) > 0.1)$protein_id){
  pepTest <- subset(pepTracesList_filtered, trace_subset_ids = i, trace_subset_type = "protein_id")
  plot(pepTest, design_matrix = design_matrix, log = FALSE, legend = F, PDF=F, name = i)
} #not saved in the nas

```

```{r filter not by pval, but by the strength of the difference}
for(i in filter(diffAssemblyState, abs(meanDiff) > 0.1)$protein_id){
  pepTest <- subset(pepTracesList_filtered, trace_subset_ids = i, trace_subset_type = "protein_id")
  plot(pepTest, design_matrix = design_matrix, log = FALSE, legend = F, PDF=F, name = i)
}
```


actually, the other way around => fix this by inverting the factor
order for ATP and ctrl. Proteins that are LESS assembled

```{r}

#get all the proteins in the list
all_elements_less_assembled_proteinslist <- unique(unlist(less_assembled_proteins_list))

# lookup all the proteins_withAssembly Changes in any of the treatment conditions

for(i in all_elements_less_assembled_proteinslist){
  pepTest <- subset(pepTracesList_filtered, trace_subset_ids = i, trace_subset_type = "protein_id")
  plot(pepTest, design_matrix = design_matrix, log = FALSE, legend = F, PDF=F, name = i) #not saved in the nas
}

```

Proteins that are MORE assembled

```{r}

#get all the proteins in the list
all_elements_more_assembled_proteinslist <- unique(unlist(more_assembled_proteins_list))

# lookup all the proteins_withAssembly Changes in any of the treatment conditions

for(i in all_elements_more_assembled_proteinslist){
  pepTest <- subset(pepTracesList_filtered, trace_subset_ids = i, trace_subset_type = "protein_id")
  plot(pepTest, design_matrix = design_matrix, log = FALSE, legend = F, PDF=F, name = i) #not saved in the nas
}

```

`

# DISTINCT assembly state ~ PROTEIN level analysis

### - filter for prot with min N pep

```{r}
## subset to keep only the proteins with at least 5 precursors 
pepTracesList_filtered_minN <- subset(pepTracesList_filtered, trace_subset_ids = uniqueProteins, trace_subset_type = "protein_id")
```

## - performProteinGridSearch

```{r perform_prot_grid_search}

if(perform_prot_grid_search == T) {
# Calculate the number of unique protein IDs to select (10% of total)
num_ids <- ceiling(length(unique(pepTracesList_filtered_minN$ctrl_1$trace_annotation$protein_id)) * 0.1)

# Randomly sample 10% of unique protein IDs
subset_ids <- sample(unique(pepTracesList_filtered_minN$ctrl_1$trace_annotation$protein_id), num_ids)

grid_peptideTraces <- subset(pepTracesList_filtered_minN,
                        trace_subset_ids = subset_ids,
                        trace_subset_type = "protein_id")

# performProteinGridSearch doesn't work with Traces List -> only work on the control list:

grid_peptideTraces <- grid_peptideTraces$ctrl_1


# Perform a small grid search for 2 parameter combinations
 # Depending on the computational resources this can take several minutes


   proteinFeatures_grid <- performProteinGridSearch(traces = grid_peptideTraces,
                                      corrs = c(0.75, 0.9, 0.95), #corr_cutoff, 0.9 for Isabel. #tried 0.5, not good 
                                      windows = c(5,7,10,12), #window_size, 7 for Isabel
                                      smoothing = c(7,9,11), #smoothing_length, 7 for Isabel 
                                      rt_heights = c(1,4,5), #rt_height, 1 for Isabel
                                      n_cores = 5) #n_cores = 3, for Isabel


 lapply(proteinFeatures_grid, head, n = 2) }
```

```{r proteinFeatures_bestStats, eval=FALSE}
if(perform_prot_grid_search == T) {
proteinFeatures_scoredData <- lapply(proteinFeatures_grid, calculateCoelutionScore)
proteinFeatures_qvalueData <- lapply(proteinFeatures_scoredData, calculateQvalue, plot=F)
proteinFeatures_stats <- qvaluePositivesPlotGrid(proteinFeatures_qvalueData)
proteinFeatures_bestStats <- getBestQvalueParameters(proteinFeatures_stats, FDR_cutoff = 0.05)
proteinFeatures_bestStats

save(proteinFeatures_grid,proteinFeatures_bestStats, file = "ProtGridSearch.RData")}

if(perform_prot_grid_search == F) {load(file = "ProtGridSearch.RData")}
#if(perform_prot_grid_search == F) {load(file = "ProtGridSearch_fromJP02.RData")} # this one is to use in case you don't want to run the gridsearch at all

```

## - findProteinFeatures

```{r}
#' # Integrate peptide traces across conditions 
# per each oe 
pepTracesIntegrated_sum <- integrateTraceIntensities(pepTracesList_filtered_minN,
                                                     design_matrix = NULL,
                                                     integrate_within = NULL,
                                                     aggr_fun = "sum")

#' # Perform protein-centric analysis
# Suppress messages for clarity

suppressMessages(
  proteinFeatures <-  findProteinFeatures(traces = pepTracesIntegrated_sum,
                                          corr_cutoff = 0.85, # 0.9,
                                          window_size = 7,# 7,
                                          parallelized = T,
                                          n_cores = 6,
                                          collapse_method = "apex_only",
                                          perturb_cutoff = "5%",
                                          rt_height = 1, #1,
                                          smoothing_length = 3 #7)

)
)

suppressMessages(
  proteinFeatures_0p7_corr_cutoff <-  findProteinFeatures(traces = pepTracesIntegrated_sum,
                                          corr_cutoff = 0.7, # 0.9,
                                          window_size = 7,# 7,
                                          parallelized = T,
                                          n_cores = 6,
                                          collapse_method = "apex_only",
                                          perturb_cutoff = "5%",
                                          rt_height = 1, #1,
                                          smoothing_length = 3 #7)

)
)

suppressMessages(
  proteinFeatures_0p5_corr_cutoff_winsize5 <-  findProteinFeatures(traces = pepTracesIntegrated_sum,
                                          corr_cutoff = 0.5, # 0.9,
                                          window_size = 5,# 7,
                                          parallelized = T,
                                          n_cores = 6,
                                          collapse_method = "apex_only",
                                          perturb_cutoff = "5%",
                                          rt_height = 1, #1,
                                          smoothing_length = 3 #7)

)
)

suppressMessages(
  proteinFeatures_0p4_corr_cutoff_winsize5 <-  findProteinFeatures(traces = pepTracesIntegrated_sum,
                                          corr_cutoff = 0.4, # 0.9,
                                          window_size = 5,# 7,
                                          parallelized = T,
                                          n_cores = 6,
                                          collapse_method = "apex_only",
                                          perturb_cutoff = "5%",
                                          rt_height = 1, #1,
                                          smoothing_length = 3 #7)

)
)


save(proteinFeatures, file = "proteinFeatures.RData")

#' Score protein features and filter for a detection FDR of 5%
proteinFeaturesFiltered <- scoreFeatures(proteinFeatures, FDR=0.05, PDF=TRUE, name="output_files/qvalueStats_proteinFeatures")

#' Evaluate protein feature stats
summarizeFeatures(proteinFeaturesFiltered,
                  plot=TRUE,
                  PDF = F)
                  # PDF=TRUE,
                  # name="output_files/feature_summary_proteinFeaturesFiltered")

#' ## Extract feature values
proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureVals <- extractFeatureVals(traces = pepTracesList_filtered_minN,
                                             features = proteinFeaturesFiltered,
                                             design_matrix = design_matrix,
                                             extract = "subunits_detected",
                                             imputeZero = T,
                                             verbose = F)


#' ## Fill feature values
proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureValsFilled <- fillFeatureVals(featureVals = proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureVals,
                                                tracesList = pepTracesList_filtered_minN,
                                                design_matrix = design_matrix)

proteinFeaturesFiltered_0p15featurefdr <- scoreFeatures(proteinFeatures, FDR=0.15, PDF=TRUE, name="output_files/qvalueStats_proteinFeatures")

#' Evaluate protein feature stats
summarizeFeatures(proteinFeaturesFiltered_0p15featurefdr,
                  plot=TRUE,
                  PDF = F)
                  # PDF=TRUE,
                  # name="output_files/feature_summary_proteinFeaturesFiltered")

#' ## Extract feature values
proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureVals_0p15featurefdr <- extractFeatureVals(traces = pepTracesList_filtered_minN,
                                             features = proteinFeaturesFiltered_0p15featurefdr,
                                             design_matrix = design_matrix,
                                             extract = "subunits_detected",
                                             imputeZero = T,
                                             verbose = F)


#' ## Fill feature values
proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureValsFilled_0p15featurefdr <- fillFeatureVals(featureVals = proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureVals_0p15featurefdr,
                                                tracesList = pepTracesList_filtered_minN,
                                                design_matrix = design_matrix)

# save(proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureVals, proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureValsFilled, file = "Anna_proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureValsFilled.RData")


```

## - differential analysis

2 cond, 1 rep function

" Differential analysis is performed in 5 steps: (i)
Peptide-levelintensities are computed for each protein feature and
sample.Missing values in single fractions, replicates, or conditions
areimputed by uniformly sampling values between zero and theminimum
detected signal of a peptide. The peptide intensity ofone feature is
then calculated by summing the intensities of allfractions across the
corresponding protein feature range. (ii)The mean intensity across all
replicates within a condition(specified by the design matrix) is
calculated. (iii) The log2-fold-change between conditions is calculated
based on themean feature intensities. (iv) If replicates are available,
p-values are estimated by comparing the summed intensities across
conditions by a non paired t test. If no replicates are
available,p-values are estimated by comparing each fraction within a
feature by a paired t test across the conditions. (v) To subsequently
derive protein-level information, the peptide-level tests are aggregated
as follows: (1) protein log2-fold-changesare derived from the median
log-2-fold change across alldetected peptides of the protein, and (2)
protein p-values are estimated by determining the fold-change adjusted
median p-value and applying a beta distribution as described by Teo
etal.43 and Suomi et al.44 (for details see the methods section). (vi)
Multiple testing correction is performed by Benjamini-Hochberg
adjustment of the protein-level p-values

```{r diff}
          
            #' ## Perform peptide-level differential expression testing for all features
          
            protein_DiffExprPep <- testDifferentialExpression_beniFix(featureVals = proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureValsFilled,
                                                              compare_between = "Condition",
                                                              level = "peptide",
                                                              measuredOnly = FALSE) #also considers the imputed fractions
            # Append the df to the list

          
            
            #' ## Aggregate differential expression results to the protein level
            protein_DiffExprProtein <- aggregatePeptideTests(protein_DiffExprPep) 
            
            protein_DiffExprPep_0p15featurefdr <- testDifferentialExpression_beniFix(featureVals = proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureValsFilled_0p15featurefdr,
                                                              compare_between = "Condition",
                                                              level = "peptide",
                                                              measuredOnly = FALSE) #also considers the imputed fractions
            # Append the df to the list

          
            
            #' ## Aggregate differential expression results to the protein level
            protein_DiffExprProtein_0p15featurefdr <- aggregatePeptideTests(protein_DiffExprPep_0p15featurefdr) 
            
protein_DiffExprProtein <- protein_DiffExprProtein %>% left_join(select(uniprot, accession, protein_name, mass, gene_names, go_f, go_p, go_c, ft_act_site, ft_binding, cc_cofactor), by=join_by(feature_id == accession))

protein_DiffExprProtein_0p15featurefdr <- protein_DiffExprProtein_0p15featurefdr %>% left_join(select(uniprot, accession, protein_name, mass, gene_names, go_f, go_p, go_c, ft_act_site, ft_binding, cc_cofactor), by=join_by(feature_id == accession))
          
save(protein_DiffExprPep, file = "protein_DiffExprPep_list.RData")
save(protein_DiffExprProtein, file = "protein_DiffExprProtein_list.RData")
save.image()

```

Global differential assessment:

In addition to the feature-specific differential analysis, global
differential assessment is performed by comparing integrated intensities
across the entire fractionation dimension, instead of restricting the
analysis to a feature-specific range. The same strategies as for
feature-specific estimation of log2-fold-changes and p-values are
performed. To assess whether the signal within a protein feature is
changing because of a global change in the protein’s expression or due
to a rearrangement of the proteins relative distribution across
different assembly states,an additional analysis step is available in
CCprofiler. Here, the relative feature-specific mass fraction (FMF) is
estimated by the fraction of the detected MS signal in the
feature-specific mass range relative to the total detected signal: FMF =
sum(intensity features)/sum(intensity global) A change in FMF is
subsequently estimated by the difference in the mean FMF across
conditions diffFMF= mean(FMF condA) - mean (FMF condB)

... check the paper for full explanation of the steps
<https://pubs.acs.org/doi/epdf/10.1021/acs.jproteome.3c00125?ref=article_openPDF>

A significant change in the FMF across conditions indicates that the
protein’s relative contribution to different distinct assembly states
has changed across the conditions, thus providing insights into protein
rewiring, which is not observable by global proteome analyses. In
contrast to complex-centric analyses, described in the following
section, protein-centric differential analysis enables the assessment of
changes in distinct protein assembly states independent of the actual
knowledge of the protein’s exact interaction partners

## - volcano plots and save lists

```{r volcano plots}
library(data.table)

#create unique df - keep as data.table
combined_protein_DiffExprPep_list <- unique(protein_DiffExprPep)

combined_protein_DiffExprProtein_list <- unique(protein_DiffExprProtein)

comparison_level <- unique(combined_protein_DiffExprPep_list$testOrder) 

# create vector list to append to
diffProteins_list <- list()
diffProteins_onlypadj_list <- list()
diffProteins_global_list <- list()

#' # Make volcano plots
for (i in comparison_level) {
  
  # Keep as data.table for plotVolcano compatibility
  protein_DiffExprPep_i <- combined_protein_DiffExprPep_list[testOrder == i]
  protein_DiffExprProtein_i <- combined_protein_DiffExprProtein_list[testOrder == i]
  
  #' General volcano plots - local
  plotVolcano(protein_DiffExprPep_i, PDF = T, pBHadj_cutoff = 0.05, FC_cutoff = 2,  
              name = paste0("output_files/pep_", i))
  plotVolcano(protein_DiffExprProtein_i, PDF = T, pBHadj_cutoff = 0.05, FC_cutoff = 2, 
              name = paste0("output_files/prot_", i))
  plotVolcano(protein_DiffExprProtein_i, PDF = F, pBHadj_cutoff = 0.05, FC_cutoff = 2, 
              name = paste0("prot_", i)) #to see here
  
  #' global
  plotVolcano(protein_DiffExprPep_i, PDF = T, pBHadj_cutoff = 0.05, FC_cutoff = 2, 
              level = "global", name = paste0("output_files/global_pep_", i))
  plotVolcano(protein_DiffExprProtein_i, PDF = T, pBHadj_cutoff = 0.05, FC_cutoff = 2, 
              level = "global", name = paste0("output_files/global_prot_", i))
  
  #' Get different proteins - using data.table syntax
  diffProteins_i <- unique(protein_DiffExprProtein_i[pBHadj < 0.05 & abs(medianLog2FC) > 1]$feature_id)
  
  diffProteins_i_onlypadj <- unique(protein_DiffExprProtein_i[pBHadj < 0.05]$feature_id)
  
  #save data
  write.table(diffProteins_i, paste0("output_files/diffProteins_", i, ".txt"),
              sep = "\t", quote = F, row.names = F, col.names = F)
  write.table(diffProteins_i_onlypadj, paste0("output_files/diffProteins_onlypadj_", i, ".txt"),
              sep = "\t", quote = F, row.names = F, col.names = F)
  
  diffProteins_global_i <- unique(protein_DiffExprProtein_i[global_pBHadj < 0.05 & abs(global_medianLog2FC) > 1]$feature_id)
  
  #save data
  write.table(diffProteins_global_i, paste0("output_files/diffProteins_global_", i, ".txt"),
              sep = "\t", quote = F, row.names = F, col.names = F)
  
  #' Volcano plots highlighting different Proteins          
  highlight_protein <- diffProteins_i
  plotVolcano(protein_DiffExprProtein_i, highlight = highlight_protein, 
              PDF = F, pBHadj_cutoff = 0.05)
  
  #append to the list
  diffProteins_list[[i]] <- diffProteins_i
  diffProteins_onlypadj_list[[i]] <- diffProteins_i_onlypadj
  diffProteins_global_list[[i]] <- diffProteins_global_i
}

```


```{r compare local vs global}

#### local vs global
for(i in Sample_id_treat) {
            
          sample_vector <- c(i, ref_condition) 
          
          #filter for just 0- and condition x
          proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureValsFilled_i <- proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureValsFilled %>%
            filter(Sample %in% sample_vector) %>%
            mutate(Sample = factor(Sample, levels = sample_vector)) %>%
            arrange(Sample)

  for(cond in diffProteins_list){
  
   testFilled_norm <- proteinFeaturesFiltered_featureValsFilled_i[feature_id %in% cond]

  local_vs_global_test <- testLocalVsGlobal(featureVals = testFilled_norm,
                                            compare_between = "Condition",
                                            plot = TRUE)
  
  # local_vs_global_test_protein <- aggregateLocalVsGlobal(local_vs_global_test)
  # 
  # local_vs_global_test_sig <- unique(local_vs_global_test_protein[pBHadj<0.05][abs(medianDiff) > 0.3]$feature_id)
  # 
  # 
  # #plot
  # for (id in local_vs_global_test_sig) {
  #   plotFeatures(feature_table = proteinFeaturesFiltered,
  #                traces = pepTracesList_filtered_minN,
  #                feature_id = id,
  #                design_matrix=sub_design_matrix,
  #                calibration=calibration,
  #                peak_area = T,
  #                legend = F,
  #                onlyBest = F,
  #                monomer_MW=T,
  #                aggregateReplicates=F)
  # }
  }
}
```


# COMPLEX level analysis

```{r}
complex_portal_Ecoli <- read.csv(here("Complex_portal_83333.tsv"), sep = 't', header = TRUE) %>%
  janitor::clean_names() 
```

## - set hypothesis

```{r}
# set hypothesis

complexHypotheses_Ecoli <- complex_portal_Ecoli %>%
  dplyr::select(x_complex_ac, recommended_name, aliases_for_complex, description, complex_properties, complex_assembly, expanded_participant_list ) %>% #only keep needed columns 
separate_rows(expanded_participant_list, sep = "|") %>% #separate each partecipant in a different row
separate(expanded_participant_list, c("protein_id", "stoichiometry"), sep = "(", convert = TRUE, remove = FALSE) %>% # separate the stoichiometry in a different column
mutate(stoichiometry = gsub(")", "", stoichiometry)) %>% #remove second braket from the stoichiometry number
 dplyr::rename("complex_id"="x_complex_ac", "complex_name"="recommended_name") # fix col names

complexHypotheses_Ecoli_minimal <- complexHypotheses_Ecoli %>%
   dplyr::select(complex_id, complex_name, protein_id ) %>%
  mutate(complex_id = str_replace(complex_id, "CPX-", "")) %>%
  as.data.table()

head(complexHypotheses_Ecoli_minimal)


binary_Ecoli <- complexHypotheses_Ecoli_minimal %>%
  dplyr::select(complex_id, protein_id) %>%
  mutate(protein_id = gsub("-PRO_.*", "", protein_id))#remove -PRO_

#### plus decoy

pathLength <- calculatePathlength(binary_Ecoli)

EcoliTargetsPlusDecoys <- generateComplexDecoys(target_hypotheses=complexHypotheses_Ecoli_minimal,
                                                dist_info=pathLength,
                                                min_distance = 3,
                                                append=TRUE)


```

## - get Complex features

```{r}
#' ## Summarize protein traces across conditions
protein_traces_sum <- integrateTraceIntensities(protein_traces_list,
                                                design_matrix = NULL,
                                                integrate_within = NULL,
                                                aggr_fun = "sum")
```

```{r}

plotSummarizedMScoverage(hypotheses = complexHypotheses_Ecoli_minimal,
                         protTraces = protein_traces_sum,
                         PDF = F)


#' ## Complex feature finding
complexFeatures <-  findComplexFeatures(traces = protein_traces_sum,
                                        complex_hypothesis = EcoliTargetsPlusDecoys,
                                        corr_cutoff = 0.9,
                                        window_size = 7,
                                        parallelized = F,
                                        n_cores = 1,
                                        collapse_method = "apex_network",
                                        perturb_cutoff = "5%",
                                        rt_height = 1,
                                        smoothing_length = 7)
```

## - filter Complex features

```{r, eval=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, cache=FALSE}

complexFeaturesBest <- getBestFeatures(complexFeatures)
```

```{r}

complexFeaturesBestFilterd <- filterFeatures(complexFeaturesBest,
                                  complex_ids = NULL,
                                  protein_ids = NULL,
                                  min_feature_completeness = NULL,
                                  min_hypothesis_completeness = NULL,
                                  min_subunits = NULL,
                                  min_peak_corr = NULL,
                                  min_monomer_distance_factor = 2)





complexFeaturesBestFilterdScored <- scoreFeatures(complexFeaturesBestFilterd, FDR=0.1, PDF=F) #,  ## at FDR 0.05 anything passes name="qvalueStats_complexFeatures")

scoredDataAll <- appendSecondaryComplexFeatures(scoredPrimaryFeatures = complexFeaturesBestFilterdScored, allFeatures = complexFeatures, peakCorr_cutoff = 0.7)


plotSummarizedComplexes(scoredDataAll, complexHypotheses_Ecoli_minimal, protein_traces_sum, PDF=F) # ,name="complex_completeness_pie")

summarizeFeatures(scoredDataAll,
                plot=TRUE,
                PDF=F ) #,name="feature_summary_scoredDataAll")

summarizeFeatures(complexFeaturesBestFilterdScored,
                  plot=TRUE,
                  PDF= F) #,  name="feature_summary_complexFeaturesBestFiltered")

```

## - extract Complex features from each pep traces

extract complex features from pep traces pepTracesList_filtered_minN

by condition

```{r}


complexFeaturesUnique <- getUniqueFeatureGroups(
                            feature_table = scoredDataAll,
                            rt_height = 0,
                            distance_cutoff = 1.25)

complexFeaturesCollapsed <- callapseByUniqueFeatureGroups(
                              feature_table = complexFeaturesUnique,
                              rm_decoys = TRUE)

################' ## Extract feature values
if(!file.exists("complex_featureVals.RData")){

complex_featureVals <- extractFeatureVals(traces = pepTracesList_filtered_minN,
                                          features = complexFeaturesCollapsed,
                                          design_matrix = design_matrix,
                                          extract = "subunits",
                                          imputeZero = T,
                                          verbose = F)

save(complex_featureVals, file = "complex_featureVals.RData")}

if(file.exists("complex_featureVals.RData")){
  load("complex_featureVals.RData")}

######################################################################################

complex_featureValsFilled <- fillFeatureVals(featureVals = complex_featureVals,
                                             tracesList = pepTracesList_filtered_minN,
                                             design_matrix = design_matrix)
```

## - differential

```{r}
# Reordering the vector


# create list to append to
complex_DiffExprPep_list <- list()

for(i in Sample_id_treat) {
            
          sample_vector <- c(i, ref_condition) 
          
          #filter for just 0- and condition x
          complex_featureValsFilled_i <- complex_featureValsFilled %>%
            filter(Sample %in% sample_vector) %>%
            mutate(Sample = factor(Sample, levels = sample_vector)) %>%
            arrange(Sample)


          #' ## Perform peptide-level differential expression testing for all features
          complex_DiffExprPep_i <- testDifferentialExpression_1repfix_chatgpt(featureVals = complex_featureValsFilled_i,
                                                            compare_between = "Condition",
                                                            level = "peptide",
                                                            measuredOnly = FALSE)
          
          # Append to the list
                    complex_DiffExprPep_list[[i]] <- complex_DiffExprPep_i
}
```

```{r}
for (i in complex_DiffExprPep_list) {
  
#plots some numbers
hist(i$pVal, breaks = 100)
    hist(i$global_pVal, breaks = 100)
    hist(log(i$qint1), breaks = 100)
    hist(log(i$qint2), breaks = 100)
    hist(log(i$global_int2_imp), breaks = 100)
    hist(log(i$global_int1_imp), breaks = 100)
    hist(i$log2FC, breaks = 50)
    hist(i$global_log2FC, breaks = 50)
}
```

```{r}
complex_DiffExprProtein_list <- list()

for (i in seq_along(complex_DiffExprPep_list)) {
  
  # Get the current dataframe
  df <- complex_DiffExprPep_list[[i]] # need to do this, because otherwise the indexing is wrong when I want to append the results to complex_DiffExprProtein_list
  
#' ## Aggregate differential expression results to the protein level
  complex_DiffExprProtein <- aggregatePeptideTests(df) # need to do this, because otherwise the indexing is wrong when I want to append the results to complex_DiffExprProtein_list


# and plot numbers
hist(complex_DiffExprProtein$pVal, breaks = 100)
    hist(complex_DiffExprProtein$global_pVal, breaks = 100)
    hist(complex_DiffExprProtein$medianLog2FC, breaks = 50)
    hist(complex_DiffExprProtein$global_medianLog2FC, breaks = 50)
    
#append to vector
    # Append to the list with name
  name <- names(complex_DiffExprPep_list)[i]
  complex_DiffExprProtein_list[[name]] <- complex_DiffExprProtein
    }
```

```{r}
complex_DiffExprComplex_list <- list()

for (i in seq_along(complex_DiffExprProtein_list)) {
  
  # Get the current dataframe
  df <- complex_DiffExprProtein_list[[i]] # need to do this, because otherwise the indexing is wrong when I want to append the results to complex_DiffExprComplex_list
  
 #' ## Aggregate differential expression results to the complex level
 complex_DiffExprComplex <- aggregateProteinTests(df)

# plot some numbers
 hist(complex_DiffExprComplex$pVal, breaks = 100)
    hist(complex_DiffExprComplex$global_pVal, breaks = 100)
    hist(complex_DiffExprComplex$medianLog2FC, breaks = 50)
    hist(complex_DiffExprComplex$global_medianLog2FC, breaks = 50)

#append to vector keeping names
  
  name <- names(complex_DiffExprProtein_list)[i]
  complex_DiffExprComplex_list[[name]] <- complex_DiffExprComplex
}
    
```

## - general volcano plots

```{r}
for(i in complex_DiffExprPep_list) {
  plotVolcano(i, PDF = F, pBHadj_cutoff = 0.05)}
```

```{r}
for(i in complex_DiffExprProtein_list) {
  plotVolcano(i, PDF = F, pBHadj_cutoff = 0.05)}
```

```{r}
for(i in complex_DiffExprComplex_list) {
  plotVolcano(i, PDF = F, pBHadj_cutoff = 0.05)}
```

## - Get differential complexes

```{r}

diffComplexes_list <- list()

for (j in seq_along(complex_DiffExprComplex_list)) {
  
  # Extract the current dataframe
  i <- complex_DiffExprComplex_list[[j]]
  
  # Identify unique complexes and differential complexes
  allComplexes <- unique(i$complex_id)
  diffComplexes <- unique(i[i$pBHadj < 0.05 & abs(i$medianLog2FC) > 1, "complex_id"])
  
  #   Append diffComplexes to the list with the name from the original list
  name <- names(complex_DiffExprComplex_list)[j]
  diffComplexes_list[[name]] <- diffComplexes
  
  # Volcano plot highlighting differential complexes
  highlight_complex <- diffComplexes
  plotVolcano(i, highlight = highlight_complex, PDF = FALSE, pBHadj_cutoff = 0.05)
}
```

## - plot diffComplexes

```{r}
  #' # Plot some differential protein features
  #' 
for (i in diffComplexes_list) {
  
    for (id in i) {
        plotFeatures(feature_table = scoredDataAll,
                     traces = protein_traces_list,
                     feature_id = id,
                     annotation_label = "input_id",
                     design_matrix=design_matrix,
                     calibration=calibration,
                     peak_area = T,
                     legend = F,
                     onlyBest = F,
                     monomer_MW=T)
    }
  }

```

## - plot all_complexes

```{r}

all_complexes <- unique(complex_featureValsFilled$complex_id)

for (id in all_complexes) {
  plotFeatures(feature_table = scoredDataAll,
               traces = protein_traces_list,
               feature_id = id,
               annotation_label = "input_id",
               design_matrix=design_matrix,
               calibration=calibration,
               peak_area = F,
               legend = T,
               onlyBest = F,
               monomer_MW=T,
               aggregateReplicates=F) #turn T if n_replicates >1
}
dev.off()
```

## save RData for plotting

